record.field: CyberChef is the "Cyber Swiss Army Knife": a browser-based workbench for encryption, encoding, compression and data analysis.

CyberChef is the "Cyber Swiss Army Knife": a browser-based workbench for encryption, encoding, compression and data analysis.

Developed and open-sourced by GCHQ, CyberChef lets you chain hundreds of operations into a visual "recipe" to transform and analyse data. Everything runs entirely client-side in the browser, so sensitive inputs never leave your machine, making it ideal for handling untrusted or confidential data. Self-hosting gives security teams a private, offline instance with no external dependencies.

Key features
  • Hundreds of operations: Base64/XOR, AES/DES/Blowfish, hashing, hexdumps and more
  • Drag-and-drop recipe builder that chains operations with live auto-bake output
  • "Magic" auto-detection that suggests how to decode or decrypt unknown data
  • Fully client-side processing, no data is sent to any server
  • Recipe breakpoints and step-through debugging for complex transformations
  • Shareable recipes via URL and a simple Docker deployment with no persistent storage
Best for

Security analysts, incident responders, malware and forensics teams, CTF players and developers who need to quickly decode, encrypt, parse or transform data without trusting an online service.

services: cyberchef: image: ghcr.io/gchq/cyberchef:latest restart: unless-stopped container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
